title: Function names should follow a naming convention
description: Function names should comply with a naming conventionAnalyze your codeconsistency-identifiablemaintainabilityCode SmellconventionWhy is this an issue?More InfoShared naming conventions allow teams to collaborate efficiently.This rule raises an issue when a function name does not match a provided regular expression.For example, with the default provided regular expression:^[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*$, the function:void DoSomething(); // Noncompliantshould be renamed tovoid doSomething();ExceptionsThe rule ignores the names of:Overriding methodsTemplate specializationsWhen an interface expects a specific name (e.g.,beginorendfor range-based loops,get<N>for structural binding, etc.)Coroutines (S6193covers them)Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps S
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: void DoSomething();  // Noncompliant: starts with capital letter
int calculate_VALue();  // Noncompliant: mixed case with underscore
compliant: void doSomething();  // Compliant: camelCase starting with lowercase
int calculateValue();  // Compliant: follows camelCase convention
void processData();   // Compliant: proper naming convention

checks.message: Function names should follow a naming convention. Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
